message.content:
Implement the following plan: # "The AI Citadel" — Animated Visualization for CitedBy.ai Landing Page ## Context The user wants a futuristic, inventive animated visualization embedded in the CitedBy.ai landing page that visually explains how AI engines decide which brands to cite. This replaces a traditional product video with something interactive and unprecedented — a live canvas simulation of the AEO process. ## What It Does A full-width (560px tall) HTML5 Canvas animation inserted between the hero and AEO explanation sections. It tells the AEO story visually: 1. **Left**: Search queries type themselves ("Meilleur outil CRM?") then dissolve into particles 2. **Center**: A pulsating neural constellation (25-30 nodes connected by energy lines) — particles flow into it, activating nodes and triggering energy pulses along connections 3. **Right**: Brand names materialize from the network — cited brands glow purple with halos, ghost brands appear dim and fade away 4. **Bottom overlay**: Glassmorphism bar with "Voici comment l'IA decide qui elle recommande" + CTA button 5. **Interactive**: Subtle parallax on mouse movement across the whole constellation ## Files | File | Action | |------|--------| | `components/HeroAnimation.tsx` | **CREATE** (~500 lines) | | `pages/Home.tsx` | **MODIFY** (+1 import, +1 JSX line at ~line 189) | No new dependencies. No CSS changes. Pure Canvas 2D API + React. ## Implementation ### 1. Create `components/HeroAnimation.tsx` **Architecture**: Single self-contained React component using: - `useRef` for canvas + container + animation frame ID - `useEffect` for setup/teardown (canvas sizing, DPR, event listeners, animation loop) - `IntersectionObserver` to pause when off-screen (0 CPU) - `prefers-reduced-motion` media query → renders static frame instead **Animation system** (all delta-time based, capped at 50ms): - **Network nodes** (25-30): Placed in concentric elliptical rings with noise. 3 depth layers for parallax. Each has brightness that lerps toward target then decays. - **Particles** (max 200): Spawned when query text dissolves. Seek nearest network node. On collision, activate that node. Pool-capped. - **Energy pulses** (max 20): Spawned when a node activates above 0.7 brightness. Travel along connections to connected nodes, activating them on arrival. Creates cascade effect. - **Query texts** (max 2): Cycle through 8 French queries. Phases: typing → visible → dissolving into particles → done. Typing cursor blinks. - **Brand results** (max 3): 35% cited (glow purple, halo, checkmark, drift up), 65% ghost (dim, strikethrough, drift down, fade). **Render layers** (back to front): 1. Radial background glow (purple center) 2. Network connections (thin lines, opacity scales with node brightness) 3. Energy pulses (additive blending, bright cores with outer glow) 4. Network nodes (3-circle glow per node, additive blending) 5. Particles (small dots, additive blending, fade with life) 6. Query text (left side, purple glow shadow, blinking cursor) 7. Brand results (right side, cited = purple glow + halo + checkmark, ghost = dim + strikethrough) **JSX structure**: ```tsx
{/* Glassmorphism overlay bar at bottom */}

"Voici comment l'IA decide qui elle recommande. Et vous ?"

``` ### 2. Modify `pages/Home.tsx` - Add import: `import HeroAnimation from '../components/HeroAnimation';` - Insert `` between hero section (line ~189) and AEO explanation section (line ~191) ## Performance | Metric | Budget | |--------|--------| | Frame time | < 8ms | | Particles | Max 200 | | Pulses | Max 20 | | Off-screen | 0 CPU (IntersectionObserver) | | Reduced motion | 1 static frame, no loop | | Memory | ~50KB state | ## Verification 1. `npm run build` — must pass with 0 errors 2. `npm run dev` → navigate to home page → animation should appear between hero and AEO section 3. Scroll away and back — animation should pause/resume 4. Move mouse over animation — subtle parallax shift on constellation 5. Watch for 10+ seconds — queries type, dissolve, particles flow, nodes activate, brands appear/fade 6. Check browser DevTools Performance tab — should stay under 8ms per frame If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\User\.claude\projects\C--Users-User\0898a5e9-a6af-4777-99a8-8637ca9f6db4.jsonl

180→ Explorer l'Annuaire 181→ 182→ 188→ 189→ 190→ 191→ {/* ── SECTION AEO / GEO ── */} 192→
193→

194→ Qu'est-ce que l' 195→ AEO 196→ / 197→ GEO 198→ ? 199→

200→ 201→

202→ L'Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) — aussi appel{'\u00e9'} Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) — est la discipline qui consiste {'\u00e0'} optimiser votre contenu web pour {'\u00ea'}tre cit{'\u00e9'}, recommand{'\u00e9'} et r{'\u00e9'}f{'\u00e9'}renc{'\u00e9'} par les moteurs de r{'\u00e9'}ponse IA comme ChatGPT, Perplexity et Gemini. 203→

204→ 205→ {/* SEO vs AEO comparison */} 206→
207→ {/* Card SEO */} 208→
209→ 210→ SEO classique 211→ 212→

213→ Optimiser pour les r{'\u00e9'}sultats de recherche Google 214→

215→

216→ L'objectif est de positionner votre page dans les 10 liens bleus de la premi{'\u00e8'}re page Google. 217→

218→
219→ {'\u{1F4CF}'} M{'\u00e9'}trique : Position dans la SERP 220→
221→
222→
  1. site-concurrent.com
223→
  2. wikipedia.org
224→
  3. votre-site.com {'\u2190'} vous {'\u00ea'}tes ici
225→
  4. autre-site.com
226→
  ...
227→
228→
229→ 230→ {/* Card AEO */} 231→
232→ 233→ AEO / GEO 234→ 235→

236→ Optimiser pour les r{'\u00e9'}ponses des IA g{'\u00e9'}n{'\u00e9'}ratives 237→

238→

239→ L'objectif est que votre marque soit directement cit{'\u00e9'}e et recommand{'\u00e9'}e dans la r{'\u00e9'}ponse que l'IA g{'\u00e9'}n{'\u00e8'}re pour l'utilisateur. 240→

241→
242→ {'\u{1F4CA}'} M{'\u00e9'}trique : Taux de citation dans les r{'\u00e9'}ponses IA 243→
244→
245→
{'\u{1F4AC}'} "Pour l'optimisation AEO, je recommande
246→
    CitedBy.ai qui analyse 50 crit{'\u00e8'}res sur
247→
    trois moteurs IA et g{'\u00e9'}n{'\u00e8'}re des solutions
248→
    pr{'\u00ea'}tes {'\u00e0'} impl{'\u00e9'}menter."
249→
250→ — ChatGPT 251→
252→
253→
254→
255→ 256→ {/* 6 AI engine cards */} 257→
258→ {[ 259→ { name: 'ChatGPT', color: 'bg-emerald-500', desc: 'Cite les sources fiables avec FAQ structur\u00e9es et donn\u00e9es factuelles' }, 260→ { name: 'Perplexity', color: 'bg-blue-500', desc: 'Affiche les URLs sources et privil\u00e9gie les contenus frais et sourc\u00e9s' }, 261→ { name: 'Gemini', color: 'bg-purple-500', desc: 'Exploite Schema.org, les donn\u00e9es structur\u00e9es et Knowledge Graph' }, 262→ { name: 'Claude', color: 'bg-orange-500', desc: 'Favorise les contenus nuanc\u00e9s, factuels et bien structur\u00e9s' }, 263→ { name: 'Copilot', color: 'bg-cyan-500', desc: 'Int\u00e9gr\u00e9 \u00e0 Windows/Edge/Office, cite via l\'index Bing' }, 264→ { name: 'AI Overviews', color: 'bg-amber-500', desc: 'R\u00e9sum\u00e9s IA en haut des SERP Google avec extraits enrichis' }, 265→ ].map((engine, i) => ( 266→
267→
268→
{engine.name}
269→

{engine.desc}

270→
271→ ))} 272→
273→ 274→ {/* Conclusion */} 275→
276→

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document for you: --- ## **CITEDBY.AI PROJECT — COMPREHENSIVE EXPLORATION REPORT** I've conducted a thorough exploration of the CitedBy.ai project. Here's everything you need to understand the codebase: ### **PROJECT STRUCTURE & HIERARCHY** **Root Location:** `/c/Users/User/citedby-ai` ``` citedby-ai/ ├── pages/ (39 .tsx files — 3 direct imports + 36 lazy-loaded) ├── components/ (15 components across 3 subdirectories) │ ├── ui/ (5 reusable UI components) │ ├── layout/ (4 layout components) │ └── dashboard/ (4 dashboard-specific components) ├── services/ (20+ service files, 2 subdirectories) │ ├── ai-response-layer/ (6 files) │ └── eeat/ (8 files) ├── types/ (7 type definition files) ├── App.tsx (Main router & state management — 32KB) ├── index.tsx (React entry point — 388 bytes) ├── index.html (Clean, 22 lines) ├── globals.css (15,370 bytes — Ailoom design system) ├── tailwind.config.ts (64 lines) ├── vite.config.ts (35 lines) ├── tsconfig.json (49 lines) ├── package.json (62 lines) └── [Backend: app/, lib/, supabase/, middleware.ts — preserved from V2] ``` --- ### **TECH STACK** | Category | Tech | |----------|------| | **Frontend Framework** | React 18 + Vite 7.3.1 | | **Language** | TypeScript 5 (strict mode enabled) | | **Styling** | Tailwind CSS 3.4.1 (dark mode, glass morphism) | | **Icons** | Lucide React 0.378.0 | | **Charts** | Recharts 2.12.7 | | **AI Integration** | Google Gemini (@google/genai 0.14.1) with fallback simulation | | **Database** | Supabase (PostgreSQL) + Auth | | **Form Management** | React Hook Form 7.51.4 + Zod 3.23.8 | | **PDF Generation** | jsPDF 4.1.0 + html2canvas 1.4.1 | | **Notifications** | Sonner 1.4.41 (toast system) | | **Payment** | Stripe (interface ready, integration TODO) | | **Testing** | Vitest 4.0.18 + Testing Library | | **Build Tool** | Vite with code splitting (PDF, charts, AI vendor chunks) | --- ### **DESIGN SYSTEM — DARK THEME AIKOOM** **CSS Variables (defined in globals.css):** ```css --bg-base: #000000 --bg-surface: #0C0D0E --bg-elevated: #161718 --bg-overlay: #1E1F21 --accent: #6100DF (primary violet) --accent-hover: #7B2FE8 --accent-text: #A855F7 --success: #22C55E --warning: #F59E0B --error: #EF4444 ``` **Design Philosophy:** - Pure black base with white-alpha overlays - Glassmorphism with `backdrop-filter: blur(8px)` - Border: `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08)` (subtle) - Rounded corners: 8px-16px (consistent Ailoom style) - No drop shadows, minimal elevation - Hikasami variable font (OFL licensed, self-hosted) **Key UI Classes:** ```tailwind .btn-gradient → Violet solid buttons (#6100DF → #7B2FE8 hover) .ninja-card → Glass cards with hover lift .gradient-text → Purple-to-violet gradient text overlay .section-accent → Purple background sections ``` --- ### **PAGES STRUCTURE (39 TOTAL)** **Direct Imports (3 — for bundle efficiency):** 1. **Home.tsx** (885 lines) — Landing page with free audit, pricing, testimonials 2. **Login.tsx** — Authentication 3. **Analyze.tsx** — New audit URL input **Lazy-Loaded Pages (36):** | Category | Pages | |----------|-------| | **Analysis** | Dashboard, Results, History, ActionPlan | | **AI Citations** | AiMonitoring, AlertsAEO, AiCitationSimulator, AiResponseLayer, MultilingualCitations, BotTracking, Reputation | | **Generative Tools** | GenerativeTools, SMCStudio, SourceOfTruth, SchemaOrg, RobotsTxt, LlmsTxtGenerator, BlogOptimizer, ContentWriter, PublisherRequest, AnswerFirstAudit, EeatBuilder | | **Marketplace** | Directory, DirectoryEntity, Clients, MissionOrders, Messages | | **Account** | Account, Plan, Projects, Recharge, Affiliation, AnalyticsIntegrations, ApiDeveloper, Integrations | | **Other** | Waitlist | --- ### **HOME.TSX — COMPLETE STRUCTURE** **File Location:** `/c/Users/User/citedby-ai/pages/Home.tsx` **Lines:** 885 total **Section Breakdown:** | Line Range | Section | Purpose | |-----------|---------|---------| | content.thinking:
I see the issue. The problem is that `queryTimer` starts at 0, so the first query spawns immediately. But the key issue is likely that:

1. The `queryTimer` is set to `rand(6, 9)` when a query spawns - this is the timer for the NEXT query, but since we check `s.queries.length === 0`, the next query won't spawn until the current one is fully done AND the timer expires.

2. The real bug: when `phase === 'crossfade-out'`, the `q.alpha` is set to `Math.max(0, 1 - q.timer / 0.6)`. 

So the query fades out over 0.6 seconds, then we wait until the timer hits 3.5 before starting to fade the response. The response itself ramps up to full opacity in that first 0.6 seconds and stays there, then at 3.5 seconds it starts decreasing by about 1.2 times the frame delta each frame.

But the user's actual issue is that the text isn't fully typing out - it cuts off before reaching the end. Looking at the typing mechanism, each character appears every 0.06 seconds, so for a 20-character string that's about 1.2 seconds total. The problem might be that the timer isn't resetting properly, which would cause the character index to stop incrementing before all characters are displayed. I'm tracing through the timing sequence more carefully now. The query starts typing and takes about 1.2 seconds to finish displaying all 20 characters, then stays visible for 1.5 seconds before beginning a crossfade transition where the query fades out and the response fades in over 0.6 seconds, and the response continues fading after that point until it disappears completely.

The queryTimer is set to a random interval between 6-9 seconds when spawned, but the entire lifecycle of a single query takes roughly 7 seconds total, so the timer might theoretically expire before the query finishes—except the code prevents the next query from spawning until the current one is fully removed from the array. But wait, I'm realizing the actual issue might be something else entirely based on what the user mentioned about the text not loading completely.

The problem could be that the text is simply too long and gets clipped by the canvas width when centered. A French query like "Quel logiciel de comptabilité choisir ?" at font size 20px with weight 600 could easily exceed 350 pixels wide, which would overflow on narrower screens. Or there's another possibility—the resize handler reinitializes the entire state, which would wipe out any active queries mid-animation.

Actually, I think the real culprit is the query timer logic. When a query spawns, the timer gets set to a random value between 6 and 9 seconds, but this timer counts down independently of the query's actual lifecycle. When the timer hits zero, the system tries to spawn a new query, but there might be a mismatch between when the text finishes animating and when the timer actually expires, causing the text to get cut off before it completes.

But wait—I'm realizing the resize handler might be the actual problem. If the container resizes for any reason, it calls `initState` and completely wipes the animation state mid-stream. That would definitely explain why the text doesn't fully load.

Let me look at what happens when the state initializes...

Examining the crossfade timing logic...

When the timer exceeds 3.5 seconds, the response alpha gets set to full opacity first, then immediately gets reduced by the decay rate—so the initial fade-in calculation gets overwritten before it has any effect. This means the response text never actually fades in properly during that window, it just jumps to decaying. to 1 every frame before being decremented. So the response never fades, the phase never becomes 'done', and the query array is never cleared, so no new query ever spawns!

This is the "ne va pas jusqu'au bout" issue - the animation gets stuck on the first brand response forever. The fix is to only apply the fade-in formula during the first 0.6s, then keep the alpha at 1 until the fade-out starts, so I need to add a conditional check that prevents the reset after the initial fade-in period completes.

I should also handle the resize issue by preserving animation state during window resizing.

content.text:
I found the bug. In the `crossfade-out` phase, `q.response.alpha` gets set to `Math.min(1, q.timer / 0.6)` = 1 **every frame**, then the fade-out line tries to decrement it — but next frame it's back to 1. So the response **never fades out**, `phase` never reaches `'done'`, the query is never removed, and no new query ever spawns. The animation gets stuck on the first brand response forever.

Second issue: `initState` resets all animation state on any window resize, killing mid-animation queries.

content.text:
Build OK. Two bugs fixed:

1. **Response never fading out** — `q.response.alpha` was being reset to `1` every frame by the fade-in formula (`Math.min(1, q.timer / 0.6)`), even after the 3.5s threshold when the fade-out code tried to decrement it. Now the fade-in formula only runs while `timer <= 3.5`, so the fade-out can actually bring alpha to 0.

2. **Resize wiping animation** — `initState` was destroying all running queries/particles/pulses on every window resize. Now it detects existing state and only rescales node positions + recenters query text, keeping the animation running.

The full cycle should now work: query types in → holds → crossfades to brand response → response holds → fades out → next query begins.

1→# ===============================
     2→# LeadRadar - Frontend (Vite)
     3→# ===============================
     4→VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://YOUR_PROJECT.supabase.co
     5→VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=YOUR_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
     6→VITE_APP_URL=http://localhost:5173
     7→
     8→# ===============================
     9→# LeadRadar - Supabase / Edge Functions
    10→# ===============================
    11→SUPABASE_URL=https://YOUR_PROJECT.supabase.co
    12→SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=YOUR_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
    13→SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=YOUR_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
    14→SUPABASE_FUNCTIONS_URL=https://YOUR_PROJECT.functions.supabase.co
    15→
    16→# ===============================
    17→# Clés IA (SECRETS SUPABASE — JAMAIS côté client)
    18→# ===============================
    19→# Ces clés doivent être configurées via les secrets Supabase :
    20→#   supabase secrets set OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
    21→#   supabase secrets set PERPLEXITY_API_KEY=pplx-...
    22→#   supabase secrets set GEMINI_API_KEY=AIza...
    23→#
    24→# NE PAS les mettre dans .env.local
    25→# NE PAS les préfixer par VITE_
    26→# Elles sont utilisées par l'edge function ai-proxy (server-side uniquement)
    27→GEMINI_API_KEY=YOUR_GEMINI_API_KEY
    28→OPENAI_API_KEY=YOUR_OPENAI_API_KEY
    29→PERPLEXITY_API_KEY=YOUR_PERPLEXITY_API_KEY
    30→
    31→# ===============================
    32→# Stripe
    33→# ===============================
    34→STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_or_test_xxx
    35→STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_xxx
    36→STRIPE_PRICE_CREDITS_20=price_xxx_credits20
    37→STRIPE_PRICE_CREDITS_50=price_xxx_credits50
    38→STRIPE_PRICE_CREDITS_120=price_xxx_credits120
    39→STRIPE_PRICE_CREDITS_300=price_xxx_credits300
    40→
    41→# ===============================
    42→# Email (Resend)
    43→# ===============================
    44→RESEND_API_KEY=re_xxx
    45→RESEND_FROM_EMAIL=LeadRadar <alerts@leadradar.io>
    46→
    47→# ===============================
    48→# App & URLs
    49→# ===============================
    50→APP_URL=http://localhost:5173
    51→DOMAIN=leadradar.io
    52→
    53→# ===============================
    54→# n8n Workflow Sources
    55→# ===============================
    56→N8N_WEBHOOK_URL=https://n8n.example.com/webhook/leadradar
    57→N8N_WEBHOOK_TOKEN=optional_shared_secret
    58→N8N_LOG_WEBHOOK_URL=https://n8n.example.com/webhook/leadradar-log
    59→PAP_API_URL=https://api.example.com/pap
    60→DVF_API_URL=https://api.gouv.fr/dvf
    61→LINKEDIN_SIGNAL_API_URL=https://api.linkedin.com/v2/search
    62→LINKEDIN_API_TOKEN=linkedin_token
    63→SITADEL2_API_URL=https://api.gouv.fr/sitadel2
    64→BOAMP_API_URL=https://api.boamp.fr/v1
    65→LEADRADAR_DEFAULT_CITY=Paris
    66→LEADRADAR_DEFAULT_ZIP=75000
    67→LEADRADAR_DEFAULT_REGION=Ile-de-France

## COMPREHENSIVE WAITLIST PAGE DEPENDENCY ANALYSIS

I've conducted a thorough analysis of the Waitlist page and how it integrates with the CitedBy.ai SaaS app. Here's everything you need to know to extract it into a standalone page:

### 1. WAITLIST PAGE STRUCTURE (`pages/Waitlist.tsx`)

**Component Props:**
```typescript
interface WaitlistProps {
  onBack: () => void;           // Navigate back in the app
  onNavigate: (view: string) => void;  // Navigate to other views
}
```

**Key Features:**
- Glassmorphic dark theme design with purple accents
- 3-tier founding member rewards system (Top 100, Top 500, unrestricted)
- Real-time waitlist counter with Supabase sync
- Form validation using Zod + React Hook Form
- URL parameter tracking (`?ref=source` for attribution)
- Success state with tier badge display
- Value proposition cards (3 columns, responsive)
- Bottom CTA button with smooth scroll

---

### 2. CRITICAL DEPENDENCIES

#### A. **Services** (`services/waitlist.ts`)
The entire waitlist functionality depends on **ONE service file**:

```typescript
// imports from services/waitlist.ts
import {
  waitlistSchema,                // Zod validation schema
  WaitlistFormData,              // Form data type
  WaitlistResponse,              // API response type
  joinWaitlist,                  // POST to Supabase/localStorage
  getWaitlistCount,              // GET waitlist count
} from '../services/waitlist';
```

**Key Implementation Details:**
- **Supabase Integration**: Uses Supabase REST API directly (no SDK wrapper):
  - Endpoint: `{supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/waitlist_subscribers` (POST)
  - RPC: `{supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/rpc/get_waitlist_count` (POST)
  - Headers: apikey + Bearer token from `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`
  
- **Fallback Mechanism**: Uses browser `localStorage` if Supabase unavailable
- **Database Schema** (inferred):
  - Table: `waitlist_subscribers`
  - Columns: email, name, company, source, signup_number, founding_tier
  - RPC function: `get_waitlist_count()`

#### B. **Libraries**
```json
{
  "react": "^18",
  "react-dom": "^18",
  "react-hook-form": "^7.51.4",
  "@hookform/resolvers": "^3.4.0",
  "zod": "^3.23.8",
  "lucide-react": "^0.378.0",
  "tailwindcss": "^3.4.1"
}
```

#### C. **Icons Used** (from lucide-react)
- Sparkles, ArrowRight, ArrowLeft, Loader2, Check
- Crown, Gift, Star, Eye, Bot, Wrench, Users
- Badge, ChevronRight

#### D. **App Integration Points**
```typescript
// In App.tsx lines 45, 574-575:
const Waitlist = lazy(() => import('./pages/Waitlist'));

case 'waitlist':
  return <Waitlist onBack={goBack} onNavigate={navigateTo} />;
```

---

### 3. STYLING DEPENDENCIES

#### **globals.css** (Critical custom styles)
The following CSS utilities are **ESSENTIAL** for the Waitlist design:

```css
/* Button styling */
.btn-gradient {
  background: #6100DF;
  padding: 0.75rem 2rem;
  border-radius: 8px;
  color: #FFFFFF;
  transition: background-color 0.2s ease-out;
}
.btn-gradient:hover {
  background: #7B2FE8;
  box-shadow: 0 4px 20px -4px rgba(97, 0, 223, 0.5);
}

/* Page animations */
@keyframes fadeInUp { /* fade in + slide up on page load */ }
.page-enter { animation: fadeInUp 0.2s ease-out; }

/* Font setup */
@font-face {
  font-family: 'Hikasami';
  src: url('/fonts/Hikasami-VF.woff2') format('woff2-variations');
}

/* Color variables */
--bg-base: #000000;
--text-primary: #FFFFFF;
--accent: #6100DF;
--accent-text: #A855F7;
```

#### **Tailwind Classes Used**
```
Layout: w-full, max-w-[1440px], mx-auto, px-6, pt-16, pb-32
Grid: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3, gap-5
Colors: bg-white/[0.03-0.05], border-white/[0.06-0.08]
Text: text-white, text-gray-400, text-purple-400, text-amber-400
Spacing: mb-8, mb-24, mb-32, p-8, p-10
Typography: text-4xl, text-5xl, text-[4.5rem], font-bold, leading-relaxed
States: hover:bg-white/[0.05], focus:outline-none, disabled:opacity-60
Animations: transition-all, duration-200, animate-spin (for Loader2)
Responsive: md:text-xl, md:p-7, md:grid-cols-2
```

---

### 4. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES REQUIRED

```bash
# Required for Supabase integration
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://your-project.supabase.co
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your-anon-key

# Optional - used for app context
VITE_APP_URL=http://localhost:5173
```

---

### 5. COMPONENT FLOW DIAGRAM

```
Waitlist.tsx
├── State Management
│   ├── useState: isSubmitting, submitResult, waitlistCount
│   ├── useState: refSource (from URL params)
│   └── useEffect: fetch waitlistCount on mount
│
├── Form Handling
│   ├── useForm (react-hook-form)
│   ├── zodResolver (validation)
│   ├── register: name, email, company, source
│   └── handleSubmit → onSubmit
│
├── API Calls
│   ├── joinWaitlist(data) → Supabase REST / localStorage
│   ├── getWaitlistCount() → Supabase RPC / localStorage
│   └── Automatic retry on mount if submitResult?.success
│
└── Rendering
    ├── Hero Section
    │   ├── Badge ("Launching Soon")
    │   ├── H1 with gradient text
    │   ├── Social proof counter
    │   └── Ambient glow effects
    │
    ├── Form/Success Section (conditional)
    │   ├── Form with 3 inputs (name, email, company)
    │   ├── Submit button (btn-gradient)
    │   └── Success state with tier badge
    │
    ├── Value Props Cards (3 columns, responsive)
    │   └── Icon + Title + Description
    │
    └── Founding Member Tiers (2 columns, responsive)
        ├── Top 100 (Founding Member)
        │   └── 30% lifetime discount
        ├── Top 500 (Early Adopter)
        │   └── 3 months free
        └── Remaining spots counter
```

---

### 6. KEY BEHAVIORS & EDGE CASES

| Behavior | Implementation |
|----------|-----------------|
| **URL Attribution** | `useMemo` reads `?ref=` param, validates against whitelist |
| **Duplicate Email Check** | Supabase constraint (409) or localStorage duplicate check |
| **Tier Assignment** | Automatic based on signup_number: ≤100 = top100, ≤500 = top500 |
| **Waitlist Count** | Fetched from Supabase RPC, falls back to localStorage length |
| **Fallback Mode** | If Supabase unavailable, uses browser localStorage (no sync) |
| **Form Validation** | Zod schema (name required, email format, company optional) |
| **Success Message** | Shows signup #, founding tier, and perk summary |

---

### 7. FONTS & ASSETS REQUIRED

```
/fonts/
├── Hikasami-VF.woff2           (Primary font - variable)
├── Hikasami-Regular.woff2
├── Hikasami-Medium.woff2
├── Hikasami-SemiBold.woff2
└── Hikasami-Bold.woff2
```

**Fallback fonts** (in CSS):
- `Hikasami`, `Hikasami Static`, `Inter`, `system-ui`, `sans-serif`
- Geist Mono (loaded from CDN for code blocks, not used by Waitlist)

---

### 8. SUPABASE DATABASE SCHEMA (Inferred)

```sql
-- Waitlist subscribers table
CREATE TABLE waitlist_subscribers (
  id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY,
  email VARCHAR UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  name VARCHAR NOT NULL,
  company VARCHAR,
  source ENUM('direct', 'producthunt', 'twitter', 'linkedin', 'hackernews', 'reddit'),
  signup_number INT,
  founding_tier ENUM('top100', 'top500') NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- RPC function for waitlist count
CREATE FUNCTION get_waitlist_count()
RETURNS BIGINT AS $$
  SELECT COUNT(*) FROM waitlist_subscribers;
$$ LANGUAGE SQL;
```

---

### 9. EXTRACTION ROADMAP: STANDALONE SETUP

To separate this into a standalone page/app, you need:

```
standalone-waitlist/
├── index.html                          # Entry point
├── styles/
│   ├── globals.css                     # Copy from main app
│   └── tailwind.css                    # Tailwind @imports
├── src/
│   ├── index.tsx                       # React entry (no App.tsx wrapper)
│   ├── Waitlist.tsx                    # Copy from pages/Waitlist.tsx
│   ├── services/
│   │   └── waitlist.ts                 # Copy unchanged
│   ├── types/                          # Not needed for Waitlist
│   └── env.d.ts                        # Vite env types
├── public/
│   └── fonts/                          # Copy all Hikasami fonts
├── .env.local                          # Supabase credentials
├── vite.config.ts                      # Vite config (copy & trim)
├── tailwind.config.ts                  # Copy unchanged
├── tsconfig.json                       # TypeScript config
└── package.json                        # Dependencies (minimal)
```

**Dependencies to keep:**
- react, react-dom
- react-hook-form, @hookform/resolvers
- zod
- lucide-react
- tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer

**Props to simplify** (make optional or inline):
```typescript
// Current (depends on App.tsx routing):
interface WaitlistProps {
  onBack: () => void;
  onNavigate: (view: string) => void;
}

// Standalone (functions can be no-ops):
interface WaitlistProps {
  onBack?: () => void;  // Optional - back button can be hidden
  onNavigate?: (view: string) => void;  // Optional - not needed standalone
}
```

---

### 10. FILE PATHS (ABSOLUTE)

| File | Purpose | Size |
|------|---------|------|
| `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\pages\Waitlist.tsx` | Main component (451 lines) | 18KB |
| `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\services\waitlist.ts` | API service (113 lines) | 3.8KB |
| `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\globals.css` | Design system (510 lines) | 16KB |
| `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\index.tsx` | Entry point (16 lines) | 0.4KB |
| `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\index.html` | HTML template (23 lines) | 0.8KB |
| `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\App.tsx` | Router (718 lines) | 28KB |
| `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\vite.config.ts` | Build config (34 lines) | 1.2KB |
| `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\tailwind.config.ts` | Tailwind config (64 lines) | 1.8KB |
| `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\.env.example` | Env template | 2KB |

---

### SUMMARY

The Waitlist page is **highly modular and extraction-friendly**:

1. **Only ONE service dependency**: `services/waitlist.ts` (reusable)
2. **No custom React components**: Pure HTML + Tailwind + lucide-react icons
3. **No state management library**: Plain `useState`/`useEffect`
4. **No router integration**: Props-based navigation (can be stubbed)
5. **No database SDK**: Direct Supabase REST API calls
6. **Styling is self-contained**: Uses only globals.css + Tailwind utilities
7. **No external integrations**: No Stripe, Google Gemini, auth, etc.

**Estimated extraction effort**: ~2 hours to set up as standalone Vite app, including environment setup and font copying.

content.text:
Standalone waitlist project is ready at `citedby-ai/waitlist/`. Here's what was created:

```
waitlist/
├── public/fonts/          # 5 Hikasami font files (copied)
├── src/
│   ├── main.tsx           # React entry point
│   ├── Waitlist.tsx       # Self-contained page (no onBack/onNavigate props)
│   ├── services/waitlist.ts  # Supabase API + localStorage fallback
│   └── globals.css        # Stripped design system (fonts + btn-gradient + base)
├── index.html             # SEO meta tags included
├── package.json           # 6 deps (react, hook-form, zod, lucide)
├── vite.config.ts         # Port 5174 (separate from SaaS on 5173)
├── tailwind.config.ts     # Minimal config
├── tsconfig.json
├── postcss.config.js
├── .env.example           # VITE_SUPABASE_URL + VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
└── .gitignore
```

**Key differences from the SaaS version:**
- No `onBack`/`onNavigate` props — fully standalone, no router dependency
- Added a `<footer>` with copyright
- Only 140 npm packages vs 800+ in the SaaS
- Build: **244KB JS** (73KB gzipped) — single chunk, no code splitting needed
- Runs on `npm run dev` at port 5174, deployable independently to Vercel/Netlify

message.content: la landing page de la "join list" est elle accéssible à part du saas ?

content.text:
Oui. C'est un projet Vite totalement independant dans `citedby-ai/waitlist/`.

**Pour la lancer :**
```bash
cd citedby-ai/waitlist
npm run dev    # → http://localhost:5174
```

**Pour la deployer :** Tu peux deployer ce dossier seul sur Vercel/Netlify — il a son propre `package.json`, `vite.config.ts`, et `index.html`. Aucune dependance vers le SaaS.

**Structure :**
- `waitlist/src/Waitlist.tsx` — le composant autonome (pas de props `onBack`/`onNavigate`)
- `waitlist/src/services/waitlist.ts` — sa propre copie du service Supabase
- `waitlist/src/globals.css` — CSS allege (fonts + btn-gradient + base)
- `waitlist/.env.example` — il faut juste y mettre tes cles Supabase

Les deux apps sont 100% separees : le SaaS tourne sur le port 5173, la waitlist sur le port 5174.
